key_risks: Dovish FOMC surprise sparks a tech/growth squeeze that leaves defensive tilt behind SPY benchmark
Hot May retail sales print on 6/17 lifts cyclicals/discretionary disproportionately
Regional banks reverse on weaker CRE/credit headlines or curve flattening
Staples/healthcare rotation unwinds if VIX continues falling and risk-on resumes

portfolio.rationale: Defensive leader showing strong recent momentum (+3.94% 7d) amid sticky headline CPI and weak consumer sentiment (44.8); hedges against FOMC week volatility.
Defensive sector with positive momentum (+1.32% 7d, +5.65% 30d) into FOMC meeting; lagged YTD provides catch-up potential.
Strong momentum (+1.93% 7d) supported by elevated oil/geopolitical backdrop and durable goods orders surge (+7.9% MoM).
Strong relative momentum (+3.39% 7d) with steepening curve and Fed hold (97% probability) reducing tail risk into FOMC.
Cash-like ballast yielding ~3.6% with zero duration risk into FOMC and Treasury auctions during the scoring window.

portfolio_rationale: With headline CPI re-accelerating to 4.2% YoY, forward P/E at 21.1, and a hawkish-hold FOMC plus Treasury auctions inside the scoring window, I tilt defensively toward sectors with positive 1-week momentum (staples, healthcare, defense) while adding regional banks for curve/breadth exposure and short Treasuries as ballast.
rationale_summary: Defensive-momentum tilt into a catalyst-heavy week (FOMC June 17, retail sales, BOE, BOJ). Avoid mega-cap tech given recent drawdown (-3% to -9% 1w in QQQ/IGV/SMH) and stretched valuations.
